summary.en: The historic and artistic soul of Bisbee, this district is built vertically against canyon walls, defined by turn-of-the-century architecture and a fiercely independent spirit.

description.en: A network of over 1,000 stairs that are the true sidewalks of Old Bisbee, connecting miners' homes on steep slopes to the gulch below.
A monument to Bisbee's resilience where you ride a mine train deep into the mountain to experience the cool, dark world of the miners who built the town.
Public art that pays tribute to the town's famous annual stair climb, embodying Bisbee's quirky, athletic, and artistic spirit.

prompt.en: Capture a photo that shows how buildings are stacked up the steep canyon walls, showcasing Bisbee's unique vertical layout.
Find a public staircase that looks like it leads to a secret garden or a hidden home and snap a picture from the bottom looking up.
Take a selfie with a steep, narrow street or a long flight of stairs in the background to show off the challenging terrain.
Discover a piece of street art, a quirky sculpture, or colorful decoration in an unexpected place and capture it.
Photograph a piece of turn-of-the-century architecture. Look for details like brickwork, cornices, or old painted signs.
The report says to explore for food. Find a local eatery that looks popular with residents and snap a picture of its inviting storefront or sign.
Follow a narrow alley or a set of stairs to see if you can find a hidden courtyard or patio space. Take a photo of your discovery.
Climb part of a public staircase and take a photo looking down into the gulch, imagining the view a miner would have had returning home.
The town is known for its bars. Find a bar or brewery with a historic facade or a unique name and photograph its entrance.
Peer up the hillsides from the main streets. Find and photograph an example of the quirky yard art mentioned in the report.
Take a close-up photo of an interesting texture you find, like crumbling concrete stairs, old painted brick, or a rusty metal railing.
Find a storefront or public space that you feel captures Bisbee's 'fiercely independent artistic spirit' and take a selfie with it.
